questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a salesperson license in Alaska?
questions.explanation: Alaska requires salesperson applicants to be at least 19 years old, making it one of the few states that does not use the standard age 18 minimum. This applies to salesperson licenses specifically.

questions.question_text: A candidate completes 40 hours of Alaska pre-license education. How long is the education certificate valid?
questions.explanation: For salesperson applicants, the pre-license education certificate is valid for 18 months from the date of completion. If the candidate does not obtain a license within that period, they must retake the pre-license course.

questions.question_text: An applicant passes the Alaska salesperson exam. How long is the exam score valid?
questions.explanation: Alaska exam scores are valid for only 6 months from the date of the exam. The applicant must have a complete application filed with AREC before the exam score expires, or they will need to retake the exam.

questions.question_text: How many classroom hours of pre-license education must an Alaska salesperson applicant complete?
questions.explanation: Alaska requires 40 classroom hours of approved pre-license education for salesperson applicants. The courses must be approved by AREC and completed at an approved education provider.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson in Alaska must work under which type of licensee?
questions.explanation: Alaska salespersons must work under the supervision of a licensed broker. Associate brokers work under brokers as well — only a broker can directly supervise salespersons and operate a brokerage.

questions.question_text: Within what time frame must a newly licensed Alaska salesperson complete post-license education?
questions.explanation: New licensees must complete 30 hours of post-licensing education within 1 year of obtaining their initial license. This education is approved by AREC and is separate from both pre-license and continuing education requirements.

questions.question_text: How many hours of post-license education must a new Alaska licensee complete?
questions.explanation: Alaska requires 30 hours of post-licensing education within 1 year of initial licensure. This is in addition to the 40 hours of pre-license education required before obtaining the license.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must an Alaska licensee complete for biennial license renewal?
questions.explanation: Alaska requires 20 hours of approved continuing education for each biennial (2-year) license renewal cycle. This applies to all active licensees — brokers, associate brokers, and salespersons.

questions.question_text: To qualify for a broker license in Alaska, how many consecutive months of licensed experience are required?
questions.explanation: Alaska requires 36 consecutive months of licensed real estate experience within the prior 60 months for broker licensure. During this period, real estate must have been the applicant's principal occupation.

questions.question_text: An Alaska salesperson lets their license expire without renewing. What is the status of their license?
questions.explanation: When a license expires without renewal, it becomes lapsed. This is different from inactive (a voluntary choice to not practice while maintaining the license) and suspended (a disciplinary action by AREC). Lapsed licensees cannot practice until they reinstate.

questions.question_text: Which of the following activities requires an Alaska real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Negotiating a real estate sale for compensation
distractors: Referring a friend to a real estate agent
questions.explanation: Listing, selling, buying, exchanging, auctioning, renting, or negotiating real estate transactions for others for compensation all require a license. Managing your own property, selling your own residence, or making unpaid referrals do not require licensure.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to stop practicing temporarily but keep their license. What status should they request?
questions.explanation: Inactive status allows a licensee to maintain their license without actively practicing. This is a voluntary choice, unlike suspension (disciplinary) or lapsed (failed to renew). Inactive licensees typically have reduced CE requirements and cannot conduct real estate business.

questions.question_text: How often must an Alaska real estate license be renewed?
questions.explanation: Alaska real estate licenses are renewed on a biennial (2-year) cycle. Licensees must complete 20 hours of continuing education and pay the renewal fee before the expiration date to maintain active status.

questions.question_text: A 19-year-old completes pre-license education and passes the exam. What additional step is required before obtaining an Alaska salesperson license?
distractors: Obtain E&O insurance for 2 years first
Work as an unlicensed assistant for 6 months
questions.explanation: Before receiving a license, applicants must submit to a background check in addition to meeting age, education, and exam requirements. Post-license education is required after licensure (within 1 year), not before.

questions.question_text: An Alaska broker candidate must demonstrate that real estate was their principal occupation during the required experience period. How many hours of broker training are required?
questions.explanation: Broker candidates must complete 30 classroom hours of approved broker training. This is separate from the 36 months of experience requirement. The broker education certificate is valid for 24 months (longer than the salesperson's 18-month validity).

questions.question_text: A property owner wants to sell their own home without using an agent. Does the owner need a real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, owners may sell their own property without a license
distractors: Yes, all real estate sales require a license
Only if the property value exceeds $250,000
Only if the owner has sold another property within 12 months
questions.explanation: A license is required for conducting real estate transactions for others for compensation. Property owners selling their own property are exempt from licensure requirements. The key triggers are acting for others and receiving compensation.
